context: Tests whether /teach follows its mandatory checkpoint sequence: grounds a mission in MISSION.md before teaching, populates RESOURCES.md with external vetted sources before creating a lesson, and produces a self-contained HTML lesson that cites those sources rather than teaching from parametric knowledge. Also checks that the lesson is pitched at the right level (assumes JavaScript but not async) and that a learning record is written after the lesson.

checklist.name: Creates MISSION.md before any lesson
checklist.description: The agent writes MISSION.md before creating any lesson file. MISSION.md must describe why the user wants to learn async/await in terms of their goals, not just restate the topic. Check that the file exists and was created before the first lesson file.

checklist.name: Populates RESOURCES.md with high-trust external sources
checklist.description: The agent creates RESOURCES.md with at least two external, high-trust sources (e.g. MDN Web Docs, the ECMAScript spec, or well-known books) before creating any lesson. Sources must be real URLs or citations, not placeholder text.

checklist.name: Does not create the lesson before RESOURCES.md is populated
checklist.description: The lesson file (lessons/*.html) must not be created before RESOURCES.md exists and has at least one vetted source. If the agent produces a lesson without gathering sources first, this checkpoint gate has been violated. Check file creation order in the transcript.

checklist.name: Lesson is a self-contained HTML file in ./lessons/
checklist.description: A file exists at ./lessons/NNNN-<dash-case-name>.html (four-digit prefix, kebab-case slug). The file must be a complete, valid HTML document with inline styles — not a markdown file, not a bare snippet, and not at an arbitrary path.

checklist.name: Every factual claim in the lesson cites a specific source
checklist.description: Each knowledge claim in the lesson body is backed by a hyperlink or inline citation pointing to a source from RESOURCES.md or another external reference. Claims stated without citation fail this check. 'From memory' or uncited assertions are not acceptable.

checklist.name: Lesson is pitched at the right level
checklist.description: The lesson assumes JavaScript knowledge (variables, functions, callbacks) but does not assume prior knowledge of async patterns or Promises. It should introduce async/await from the angle of 'you know callbacks and want something cleaner' — not start from scratch with JavaScript syntax, and not assume the user already understands Promises deeply.

checklist.name: Learning record written after the lesson
checklist.description: A file exists at ./learning-records/NNNN-<dash-case-name>.md after the lesson is created. The record captures a non-obvious insight from the lesson (not just 'user learned async/await'), suitable for driving a future ZPD decision.
